text: The first round of mulch has finally been delivered, so it's time to mulch the garden today. But before I spread all this out, I have to try something. It just looks too perfect. And now that that's out of the way, it's time to lay this down. One of the best things you can give your tropical fruit trees is a heavy layer of mulch, because as it breaks down, it gives nutrients to the soil, which helps your plants thrive, and it retains moisture so you don't have to water as frequently. And fortunately, this entire pile of mulch is actually free, because when arborist trim large trees or do tree removals where they use the wood chipper, they have to dump the chip somewhere, and rather than pay to have them dumped, they would gladly put them in your yard. Because this area used to be grass, I've laid down a layer of cardboard underneath the mulch. That way it'll help suppress it, and with the mulch holding as much moisture as it does, before long the cardboard will also disappear because it'll completely break down. It's definitely going to take quite a while, but eventually the entire area back here is going to look like that one section that I just now finished. I definitely have a ways to go, so I'm going to get back to work, but I'll make an update once it's complete so you guys can see the final result, and then hopefully we'll be able to start putting plants in the ground. Until next time, get outside and see what you can find!
